sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is created lazily upon first access.
sketches.resonance: Declaring someone as an `object` means the language itself guarantees there will never be another — scarcity enforced by the type system.

sketches.mechanism: A LEFT JOIN preserves every row from the left table even when no matching row exists on the right, filling unmatched columns with NULL.
sketches.resonance: One side stays whole; the other dissolves into absence — the join keeps showing up even when there is nothing left to meet it.
sketches.draft:
SELECT me.day, you.reply
FROM   me
LEFT JOIN you
  ON me.day = you.day;

sketches.mechanism: The `finally` block executes regardless of whether the `try` succeeds or the `catch` fires — it runs even if you return early or throw again.
sketches.resonance: No matter what went wrong, no matter what was caught or missed, the finally block still happens — grief that outlasts every possible outcome.
